Heading: Defence Committee statement: developments in the Middle East
Teaser: Defence Committee statement: developments in the Middle East

Heading: Defence and Public Accounts Committee Chairs: delay to DIP publication “risks sending damaging signals to adversaries”
Teaser: Chair of the Defence Committee, Tan Dhesi MP, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown MP, have written a joint letter to the Permanent Secretary at the Ministry of Defence (MOD), Jeremy Pocklington, seeking clarity on when the Defence Investment Plan (DIP) will be published.

Heading: New inquiry: Defence in the High North
Teaser: Melting polar ice caps are increasing access to the Arctic and High North region, with significant defence and security implications for the UK, and recent weeks have seen increased global focus on the strategic importance of the region.

Heading: Defence Committee Chair responds to President Trump’s “belittlement of the contribution of the UK and other NATO allies”
Teaser: The Defence Committee has issued a statement responding to the President Trump’s comments on the contribution of the UK and other NATO allies in Afghanistan.

Heading: Defence Secretary to face Committee questions
Teaser: John Healey, the Secretary of State for Defence, will give evidence to the Defence Committee at 2pm on Tuesday 27 January.

Heading: Planning reforms have “numerous safeguards”, Government insists
Teaser: Government response: Environmental Sustainability and Housing Growth

Heading: Fairness must drive Seventh Carbon Budget plans, MPs tell Government
Teaser: Fairness is “fundamental” to the legitimacy of the Seventh Carbon Budget and failure to deliver even benefits to all could put public consent at risk, MPs have warned in a report launched today.

Heading: MPs to examine data centres climate impact as Miliband says future demand “inherently uncertain”
Teaser: MPs have today launched a new inquiry on the environmental impacts of data centres in the UK

Heading: Environment Secretary responds to MPs on flood protection plans
Teaser: The Environmental Audit Committee has published a letter from Secretary of State for the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, Emma Reynolds, regarding the Committee’s report on flood resilience.

Heading: EAC Chair responds to Water White Paper
Teaser: EAC Chair responds to Water White Paper

Heading: MPs to investigate air pollution in next Committee inquiry
Teaser: Tackling air pollution will be the subject of a new inquiry by MPs on the Environmental Audit Committee, launched today.

Heading: “Concrete results” needed on environmental commitments – EAC Chair responds to latest Office for Environmental Protection report
Teaser: In response to the Office for Environmental Protection’s latest progress report, the Chair of the Environmental Audit Committee says the Government needs to match ambition with concrete results.
